channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: China-linked Red Menshen APT deploys stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom networks
item.description: China-linked Red Menshen APT group used stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom networks to spy on government targets. Rapid7 Labs uncovered a China-linked threat group known as Red Menshen has been running a long-term e...

item.title: AI frenzy feeds credential chaos, secrets leak through code, tools, and infrastructure
item.description: Code keeps moving through pipelines, and credentials continue to surface alongside it. GitGuardian’s State of Secrets Sprawl 2026 puts the count at 28.65 million new hardcoded secrets in public GitHub commits in 2025,...

item.title: Make OpenAI’s models misbehave and earn a reward
item.description: OpenAI’s public Safety Bug Bounty program focuses on AI abuse and safety risks across its products. The goal is to support safe and secure systems and reduce the risk of misuse that could lead to harm. This program co...

item.title: Top product launches at RSAC 2026
item.description: RSAC 2026 showcased a wave of innovation, with vendors unveiling technologies poised to redefine cybersecurity. From AI-powered defense to breakthroughs in identity protection, this year’s conference delivered a glimp...

item.title: Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation of Cyber Risk Related to Iran (Updated March 26)
item.description: Unit 42 details recent Iranian cyberattack activity, sharing direct observations of phishing, hacktivist activity and cybercrime. We include recommendations for defenders. The post Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation...

item.title: Ajax football club hack exposed fan data, enabled ticket hijack
item.description: Dutch professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) disclosed that a hacker exploited vulnerabilities in its IT systems and accessed data belonging to a few hundred people. [...]

item.title: Automotive Cybersecurity Threats Grow in Era of Connected, Autonomous Vehicles
item.description: More than a decade since the 2015 Jeep hack, the cybersecurity of vehicles remains of the utmost importance.

item.title: CISA: New Langflow flaw actively exploited to hijack AI workflows
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability identified as CVE-2026-33017, which affects the Langflow framework for building AI a...

item.title: Critical Flaw in Langflow AI Platform Under Attack
item.description: Threats actors pounced on the code injection vulnerability within hours of its disclosure, demonstrating that organizations have little time to address critical bugs.

item.title: Coruna exploit reveals evolution of Triangulation iOS exploitation framework
item.description: Kaspersky found Coruna iOS exploits reuse updated code from the 2023 Operation Triangulation attacks, suggesting a possible link. Kaspersky researchers discovered that the Coruna iOS exploit kit uses an updated versio...

item.title: Attackers Rapidly Weaponize Critical Oracle WebLogic RCE, Honeypot Study Finds
item.description: Attackers rapidly exploited a critical Oracle WebLogic RCE flaw the same day exploit code was released, according to a CloudSEK honeypot study

item.title: UK sanctions Xinbi marketplace linked to Asian scam centers
item.description: The United Kingdom's Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) has sanctioned Xinbi, a Chinese-language cryptocurrency-based online marketplace that sells stolen data and satellite internet equipment to scam...

item.title: How Organizations Can Use Blunders to Level Up Their Security Programs
item.description: Organizations repeatedly expose ports, reuse passwords, and skip patches, creating security gaps that attackers exploit for breaches. An industry veteran outlines ways to fix these common mistakes.

item.title: EtherRAT Techniques Bypass Security Via Ethereum Smart Contracts
item.description: EtherRAT hides C2 in Ethereum smart contracts via EtherHiding, steals wallets and credentials

item.title: AI-Powered Dependency Decisions Introduce, Ignore Security Bugs
item.description: AI models often hallucinate or make costly mistakes when tasked with recommending software versions, upgrade paths, and security fixes — leading to significant technical debt.

item.title: TikTok for Business accounts targeted in new phishing campaign
item.description: Threat actors are targeting TikTok for Business accounts in a phishing campaign that prevents security bots from analyzing malicious pages. [...]

item.title: Second RedLine infostealer operator ends up in US custody
item.description: Hambardzum Minasyan, an Armenian man extradited to the United States, is accused of conspiring with others to develop and operate the RedLine infostealer malware used to steal sensitive data, including login credentia...

item.title: Hightower Holding Data Breach Impacts 130,000
item.description: The holdings company says hackers stole names, Social Security numbers, and driver’s license numbers from its environment. The post Hightower Holding Data Breach Impacts 130,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: WhatsApp rolls out more AI features, iOS multi-account support
item.description: WhatsApp is rolling out multiple features designed to make the app easier to use, including AI-powered message replies and photo retouching, support for two accounts on iOS, and chat history transfer between iOS and A...

item.title: Inside a Modern Fraud Attack: From Bot Signups to Account Takeovers
item.description: Multi-stage fraud attacks chain bots, proxies, and stolen credentials from signup to takeover. IPQS shows why correlating IP, device, identity, and behavior is critical to stop it. [...]

item.title: BIND Updates Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities
item.description: Specially crafted domains could be used to cause out-of-memory conditions, leading to memory leaks in the BIND resolvers. The post BIND Updates Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: AI Becomes the Top Cybersecurity Priority for Defenders as Criminals Exploit It, PwC Warns
item.description: PwC Annual Threat Dynamics report says AI-threats are the biggest concern of clients

item.title: Claude Extension Flaw Enabled Zero-Click XSS Prompt Injection via Any Website
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Google Chrome Extension that could have been exploited to trigger malicious prompts simply by visiting a web page. The flaw "allowed any w...

item.title: Coruna iOS exploit framework linked to Triangulation attacks
item.description: The Coruna exploit kit is an evolution of the framework used in the Operation Triangulation espionage campaign, which in 2023 targeted iPhones via zero-click iMessage exploits. [...]

item.title: A one-line Kubernetes fix that saved 600 hours a year
item.description: When we investigated why our Atlantis instance took 30 minutes to restart, we discovered a bottleneck in how Kubernetes handles volume permissions. By adjusting the fsGroupChangePolicy, we reduced restart times to 30...

item.title: Introducing the Green Agent: AI-Powered Remediation for the Cloud
item.description: Accelerate your path to Zero Criticals with AI that investigates, assigns, and guides cloud remediation for you

item.title: Masters of Imitation: How Hackers and Art Forgers Perfect the Art of Deception
item.description: Unmasking impostors is something the art world has faced for decades, and there are valuable lessons from the works of Elmyr de Hory that can apply to the world of defensive cybersecurity. During the 1960s, de Hory ga...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PQC Push, AI Vuln Hunting, Pirated Traps, Phishing Kits & 20 More Stories
item.description: Some weeks in security feel loud. This one feels sneaky. Less big dramatic fireworks, more of that slow creeping sense that too many people are getting way too comfortable abusing things they probably shouldn’t even b...

item.title: Dell and HP Roll Out Quantum-Resistant Device Security
item.description: The computer giants have announced new security capabilities for PCs and printers. The post Dell and HP Roll Out Quantum-Resistant Device Security appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: WebRTC Skimmer Bypasses CSP to Steal Payment Data from E-Commerce Sites
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new payment skimmer that uses WebRTC data channels as a means to receive payloads and exfiltrate data, effectively bypassing security controls. "Instead of the usual HTTP re...

item.title: AI infrastructure security: Why it deserves its own category
item.description: Attacks on artificial intelligence infrastructure are rising, but not in the way most people expect. While AI security headlines focus on prompt manipulation, attackers are going after the infrastructure behind these...

item.title: Three pillars for building effective runtime-powered cloud defense, the right way
item.description: To secure your cloud, you have to secure it at runtime. Here’s how to build a runtime-powered cloud defense that secures your infrastructure in real time.

item.title: Closing the cloud security gap with runtime security
item.description: Imagine your cloud environment as a commercial plane. Before flight, planes undergo strict maintenance schedules, tests, and preflight checks to…

item.title: Apple Patches (almost) everything again. March 2026 edition., (Wed, Mar 25th)
item.description: Apple released the next version of its operating system, patching 85 different vulnerabilities across all of them. None of the vulnerabilities are currently being exploited. The last three macOS "generations" are cove...

item.title: Bubble AI app builder abused to steal Microsoft account credentials
item.description: Threat actors are evading phishing detection in campaigns targeting Microsoft accounts by abusing the no-code app-building platform Bubble to generate and host malicious web apps. [...]

item.title: Cloud Phones Linked to Rising Financial Fraud Threat
item.description: Cloud Android phones fuel financial fraud, evading detection and enabling dropper accounts

item.title: Onit Security Raises $11 Million for Exposure Management Platform
item.description: The startup will invest in product development and go-to-market efforts as it expands into new sectors. The post Onit Security Raises $11 Million for Exposure Management Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Citrix urges admins to patch NetScaler flaws as soon as possible
item.description: Citrix has patched two NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway vulnerabilities, one of which is very similar to the CitrixBleed and CitrixBleed2 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks in recent years. [...]

item.title: Hackers Exploit Compromised Enterprise Identities at Industrial Scale, Warns SentinelOne
item.description: Cybersecurity company’s annual report issues warning over a “mass-marketed impersonation crisis” over attackers abusing legitimate credentials

item.title: Patch now: TP-Link Archer NX routers vulnerable to firmware takeover
item.description: TP-Link patched a high severity flaw (CVE-2025-15517) in Archer NX routers that could let attackers bypass authentication and install malicious firmware. TP-Link issued security updates for its Archer NX router series...

item.title: Russian Cybercriminal Gets 2-Year Prison Sentence in US
item.description: Ilya Angelov was a member of the cybercrime group tracked as TA-551, Shathak, Gold Cabin, Monster Libra, and ATK236. The post Russian Cybercriminal Gets 2-Year Prison Sentence in US appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Uses Solana Dead Drops to Deliver RAT and Steal Browser, Crypto Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new evolution of the GlassWorm campaign that delivers a multi-stage framework capable of comprehensive data theft and installing a remote access trojan (RAT), which deploys an...

item.title: AI Speeds Attacks, But Identity Remains Cybersecurity’s Weakest Link
item.description: PwC finds AI is amplifying speed and scale of attacks, as identity theft evolves into a cybercriminal supply chain. The post AI Speeds Attacks, But Identity Remains Cybersecurity’s Weakest Link appeared first on Secur...

item.title: Paid AI Accounts Are Now a Hot Underground Commodity
item.description: AI accounts are becoming part of the cybercrime supply chain, sold like email accounts or VPS access. Flare Systems shows how underground markets bundle and resell premium AI access at scale. [...]

item.title: iOS, macOS 26.4 Roll Out With Fresh Security Patches
item.description: Apple released security fixes for older devices as well, in iOS 18.7.7, iPadOS 18.7.7, macOS Sequoia 15.7.5, and macOS Sonoma 14.8.5. The post iOS, macOS 26.4 Roll Out With Fresh Security Patches appeared first on Sec...

item.title: Credential‑Stealing Malware in LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Executive Summary A severe malware incident (no formal CVE yet, but tracked as a high‑risk supply chain compromise) was disclosed affecting the widely used Python package LiteLLM (PyPI). Attackers from the TeamPCP thr...

item.title: Operation Henhouse Nets Over 500 Arrests in UK Fraud Crackdown
item.description: UK police trumpet success of Operation Henhouse as they seize and freeze over £27m in suspected fraud proceeds

item.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over Supply Chain and Cyber Risk Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it was banning the import of new, foreign-made consumer routers, citing "unacceptable" risks to cyber and national security. The action was designed...

item.title: How Charlotte AI AgentWorks Fuels Security's Agentic Ecosystem
Seeing risk isn’t stopping it: Why visibility alone isn’t enough
item.description: Cloud security has evolved beyond basic visibility.For years, security teams have focused on understanding what exists in their environment, often playing catch-up to development teams that had already embraced the sp...

item.title: RSA Conference: UK NCSC Head Urges Industry to Develop Vibe Coding Safeguards
item.description: The head of the UK’s NCSC is calling the cybersecurity industry to “seize the disruptive vibe coding opportunity” to make software more secure

item.title: Three’s a Crowd: TeamPCP trojanizes LiteLLM in Continuation of Campaign
item.description: LiteLLM is the latest victim of TeamPCP’s open-source attack spree. Malicious versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 abuse Python’s .pth mechanism for stealthy persistence. The malware exfiltrates cloud credentials, CI/CD secrets...

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential harvester, a Kubernetes lat...

item.title: Silver Fox Cyber Campaigns Show Shift Toward Dual Espionage
item.description: Silver Fox pivots from ValleyRAT tax lures to WhatsApp‑style stealers, blending espionage & phishing

item.title: Citrix Urges Immediate Patching for Critical NetScaler Vulnerabilities
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Citrix’s NetScaler products allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak information from the appliance's memory

item.title: New Npm 'Ghost Campaign' Uses Fake Install Logs to Hide Malware
item.description: Ghost npm campaign fakes install logs to steal sudo passwords and drop RATs that loot crypto and data

item.title: Former Ukrainian Foreign Minister Dmytro Kuleba to Address the New Cyber Frontline at Infosecurity Europe
item.description: Geopolitics and cyber warfare take center stage at Infosecurity Europe as Dmytro Kuleba discusses Ukraine’s hybrid war experience

item.title: Detecting IP KVMs, (Tue, Mar 24th)
item.description: I have written about how to&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;use IP KVMs securely, and recently, researchers at Eclypsium published yet another report on IP KVM vulnerabilities.&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;But there is another i...

item.title: Enterprise Cybersecurity Software Fails 20% of the Time, Warns Absolute Security
item.description: Poor patch management, increasingly complex IT environments and continued use of obsolete software puts organizations at risk from cyber threats, says the Absolute Security 2026 Resilience Risk Index

item.title: Sandboxing AI agents, 100x faster
item.description: We’re introducing Dynamic Workers, which allow you to execute AI-generated code in secure, lightweight isolates. This approach is 100 times faster than traditional containers, enabling millisecond startup times for AI...

item.title: The Orca Approach to Runtime AI Security
item.description: The Runtime Gap: Why AI Security Can’t Stop at Posture Most AI security conversations in 2025 centered on posture. What models are deployed? Who has access? Are your AI pipelines misconfigured? These are the right que...

item.title: U.S. Sentences Russian Hacker to 6.75 Years for Role in $9M Ransomware Damage
item.description: A 26-year-old Russian citizen has been sentenced in the U.S. to 6.75 years (81 months) in prison for his role in assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware crew, in conducting numerous atta...

item.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
item.description: Citrix has released security updates to address two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway, including a critical flaw that could be exploited to leak sensitive data from the application. The vulnerabil...

item.title: Tool updates: lots of security and logic fixes, (Mon, Mar 23rd)
item.description: So, I&#;x26;#;39;ve been slow to get on the Claude Code/OpenCode/Codex/OpenClaw bandwagon, but I had some time last week so I asked Claude to review (/security-review) some of my python scripts. He found more than I&#...

item.title: IAM policy types: How and when to use them
item.description: June 3, 2022: Original publication date of this post. This post has been updated to add the additional IAM policy types: Resource control policies. You manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to A...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Abuse VS Code Auto-Run Tasks to Deploy StoatWaffle Malware
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the Contagious Interview campaign, also tracked as WaterPlum, have been attributed to a malware family tracked as StoatWaffle that's distributed via malicious Microsoft Visual Stu...

item.title: KICS GitHub Action Compromised: TeamPCP Strikes Again in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Checkmarx KICS scanner is the latest victim of a credential-stealing supply chain attack by TeamPCP. Between 12:58–16:50 UTC on March 23, 35 tags were hijacked. Learn how to audit your workflows, identify malicious ac...

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use...

item.title: M-Trends 2026: Data, Insights, and Strategies From the Frontlines
item.description: Every year, the cyber threat landscape forces defenders to adapt to evolving adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs). In 2025, Mandiant observed a clear divergence in adversary pacing that closely aligns...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: CI/CD Backdoor, FBI Buys Location Data, WhatsApp Ditches Numbers & More
item.description: Another week, another reminder that the internet is still a mess. Systems people thought were secure are being broken in simple ways, showing many still ignore basic advisories. This edition covers a mix of issues: su...

item.title: Inside Gen 13: how we built our most powerful server yet
item.description: Cloudflare's Gen 13 servers introduce AMD EPYC™ Turin 9965 processors and a transition to 100 GbE networking to meet growing traffic demands. In this technical deep dive, we explain the engineering rationale behind ea...

item.title: Launching Cloudflare’s Gen 13 servers: trading cache for cores for 2x edge compute performance
item.description: Cloudflare’s Gen 13 servers double our compute throughput by rethinking the balance between cache and cores. Moving to high-core-count AMD EPYC ™ Turin CPUs, we traded large L3 cache for raw compute density. By runnin...

item.title: We Found Eight Attack Vectors Inside AWS Bedrock. Here's What Attackers Can Do with Them
item.description: AWS Bedrock is Amazon's platform for building AI-powered applications. It gives developers access to foundation models and the tools to connect those models directly to enterprise data and systems. That connectivity i...

item.title: Trivy Hack Spreads Infostealer via Docker, Triggers Worm and Kubernetes Wiper
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered malicious artifacts distributed via Docker Hub following the Trivy supply chain attack, highlighting the widening blast radius across developer environments. The last known cle...

item.title: TeamPCP expands: Supply chain compromise spreads from Trivy to Checkmarx GitHub Actions
AI coding agents are running on your machines — Do you know what they're doing?
item.description: AI coding agents are now running on developer laptops and inside CI/CD pipelines across every sector. They write code, execute commands, read files, and make network connections, often without the developer watching....

item.title: Runtime security for AI coding agents: Protecting AI-assisted development
item.description: Use of AI coding agents is skyrocketing as organizations look to innovate and solve business problems faster. At the same time, coding agents like Claude Code, OpenAI’s Codex, and Gemini CLI, raise new questions about...

item.title: How runtime insights power every cloud security use case
item.description: Runtime insights give security teams live visibility into what’s risky and what’s being exploited in the moment. That’s why they’re critical to every use case.

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Hackers Target Signal, WhatsApp in Mass Phishing Attacks
item.description: Threat actors affiliated with Russian Intelligence Services are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications (CMAs) like WhatsApp and Signal to seize control of accounts belonging to i...

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages
item.description: The threat actors behind the supply chain attack targeting the popular Trivy scanner are suspected to be conducting follow-on attacks that have led to the compromise of a large number of npm packages with a previously...

item.title: CISA Flags Apple, Craft CMS, Laravel Bugs in KEV, Orders Patching by April 3, 2026
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added five security flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, urging federa...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jumbo Flying Squid in the South Pacific
item.description: The population needs better conservation. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: AI Runtime Threat Detection: From Input to Real-World Impact
item.description: Understanding and detecting AI-driven behavior across model, workload, and cloud

item.title: Powering the agents: Workers AI now runs large models, starting with Kimi K2.5
item.description: Kimi K2.5 is now on Workers AI, helping you power agents entirely on Cloudflare’s Developer Platform. Learn how we optimized our inference stack and reduced inference costs for internal agent use cases.

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to surreptitiously harvest sens...

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 35 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 35 vulnerable drivers. EDR kill...

item.title: Ransomware Affiliate Exposes Details of 'The Gentlemen' Operation
item.description: Hastalamuerte leaks The Gentlemen RaaS ops: FortiGate exploits, BYOVD evasion, Qilin split tactics

item.title: Financial Brands Targeted in Global Mobile Banking Malware Surge
item.description: Mobile banking malware targets over 1200 financial apps globally, shifting fraud to user devices

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: FortiGate RaaS, Citrix Exploits, MCP Abuse, LiveChat Phish & More
item.description: ThreatsDay Bulletin is back on The Hacker News, and this week feels off in a familiar way. Nothing loud, nothing breaking everything at once. Just a lot of small things that shouldn’t work anymore but still do. Some o...

item.title: New Perseus Android Banking Malware Monitors Notes Apps to Extract Sensitive Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Android malware family called Perseus that's being actively distributed in the wild with an aim to conduct device takeover (DTO) and financial fraud. Perseus is built upo...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-20131 Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software and Cisco Sec...

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it operates entirely outsid...

item.title: FCA Updates Cyber Incident and Third-Party Reporting Rules
item.description: The UK’s financial regulator has issued new rules to make incident and third-party reporting clearer

item.title: Everyday tools, extraordinary crimes: the ransomware exfiltration playbook
item.description: Attackers use trusted tools for data theft, making traditional detection unreliable. The Exfiltration Framework enables defenders to spot exfiltration by focusing on behavioral signals across endpoints, networks, and...

item.title: Building an Adversarial Consensus Engine | Multi-Agent LLMs for Automated Malware Analysis
item.description: Single-tool LLM analysis produces reports that look authoritative but aren't. A serial consensus pipeline catches artifacts and hallucinations at source.

item.title: Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware
item.description: Unit 42 research explores how AI is currently used in malware, from superficial integrations to advanced decision-making, and its future impact. The post Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware appeared first...

item.title: AWS Warns Hackers Have Abused Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Since January
item.description: Notorious ransomware group Interlock has been exploiting a Cisco zero-day bug since January, AWS says

item.title: Hacking a Robot Vacuum
item.description: Someone tries to remote control his own DJI Romo vacuum, and ends up controlling 7,000 of them from all around the world. The IoT is horribly insecure, but we already knew that .

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Uses 6 Flaws, 3 Zero-Days for Full Device Takeover
item.description: A new exploit kit for Apple iOS devices designed to steal sensitive data from is being wielded by multiple threat actors since at least November 2025, according to reports from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG),...

item.title: UK: Regulation Drives Cyber Spending for Critical Infrastructure Orgs
item.description: 35% of security leaders working in the UK’s critical infrastructure said regulatory requirements are the primary influence on their security programs

item.title: CISA Warns of Zimbra, SharePoint Flaw Exploits; Cisco Zero-Day Hit in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has urged government agencies to apply patches for two security flaws impacting Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) and Microsoft Office SharePoint...

item.title: Secure Homegrown AI Agents with CrowdStrike Falcon AIDR and NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
CVE-2026-33017: How attackers compromised Langflow AI pipelines in 20 hours

item.title: Inline Cloud Response: Accelerating AWS threat containment for SOC teams
item.description: In the cloud, visibility alone is not enough. Security teams need the ability to move from detection to investigation to containment without friction, delay, or tool switching. Sysdig Cloud Response transforms the tra...

item.title: Linux & Cloud Detection Engineering - Getting Started with Defend for Containers (D4C)
item.description: This technical resource provides a comprehensive walkthrough of Elastic’s Defend for Containers (D4C) integration, covering Kubernetes-based deployment, the analysis of BPF-enriched runtime telemetry, and the practica...

item.title: Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agents
item.description: Unit 42 outlines the risks of AI ecosystems and allowing AI agents excessive privileges. Learn how to keep your security strategy up to date with these latest trends. The post Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agent...

item.title: C2 Implant 'SnappyClient' Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: In addition to enabling remote access, the malware supports a wide range of capabilities, including data theft and spying.

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
item.description: A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.

item.title: OFAC Sanctions DPRK IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs Through Fake Remote Jobs
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their involvement in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) information techn...

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day CVE-2026-20131 for Root Access
item.description: Amazon Threat Intelligence is warning of an active Interlock ransomware campaign that's exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software. The vulnerabili...

item.title: Amazon threat intelligence teams identify Interlock ransomware campaign targeting enterprise firewalls
item.description: Amazon threat intelligence has identified an active Interlock ransomware campaign exploiting CVE-2026-20131, a critical vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software that could allow an unaut...

item.title: New Ubuntu Flaw Enables Local Attackers to Gain Root Access
item.description: CVE-2026-3888 Ubuntu snap flaw lets local users escalate to root via timing-based exploit

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description: A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks.

item.title: Crypto Scam "ShieldGuard" Dismantled After Malware Discovery
item.description: ShieldGuard Chrome extension posed as a crypto security tool but stole wallets and drained user data

item.title: The Proliferation of DarkSword: iOS Exploit Chain Adopted by Multiple Threat Actors
item.description: Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a new iOS full-chain exploit that leveraged multiple zero-day vulnerabilities to fully compromise devices. Based on toolmarks in recovered payloads,...

item.title: SideWinder Espionage Campaign Expands Across Southeast Asia
item.description: The suspected India-linked threat group targets governments, telecom, and critical infrastructure using spear-phishing, old vulnerabilities, and rapidly rotating infrastructure to maintain persistent access.

item.title: Researchers: Meta, TikTok Steal Personal & Financial Info When Users Click Ads
item.description: Tracking pixels let social media companies spy on their users even after they click over to advertiser sites, gleaning credit card info, geolocations, and more, according to an analysis.

item.title: AI-Enabled Adversaries Compress Time-to-Exploit Following Vulnerability Disclosure
item.description: Rapid7 says median time from publication to CISA KEV inclusion dropped to five days

item.title: Introducing the Agentic Cloud Security Platform
item.description: Orca’s AI agents: Ecosystem engineers for cloud-native apps Ecosystem engineers are living things—animals, plants, insects, etc—that physically modify their environment in ways that create, maintain, or destroy habita...

item.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw impacting the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon (telnetd) that could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code with elevat...
